understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to move selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+') in each level to eventually win the whole puzzle game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Clicking on a grid cell attempts to move the currently selected piece to that cell. Movement is instantaneous teleportation (no sliding). If the target cell is blocked or unreachable, the piece does not move. The selection of a piece likely happens by clicking on it first, and then subsequent clicks direct its movement. In the observed turns, a piece repeatedly moved between two positions when the same cell was clicked, indicating that the piece might automatically return to a previous position or that there was a back-and-forth interaction – possibly due to the piece being able to move in a straight line and the target cell being adjacent, causing a pull-push effect. The movement is constrained; clicking a distant cell like [35,17] when steps were exhausted resulted in no movement, but the failure may also be due to running out of steps.
